questionText: A drone moves so that, $t$ seconds after launch, its position in metres is modelled by $x = t^{2} - 1$ and $y = t^{3} - 3t$. Find the equation of the tangent to the drone's path at the instant $t = 2$, giving your answer in the form $ax + by + c = 0$.

steps.workingLatex: \text{tangent: gradient } \dfrac{dy}{dx} \text{ and the point at } t=2
steps.explanation: A tangent line is fixed by two things: the point it touches and the gradient of the path there. So we need the coordinates at $t = 2$ and the value of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ at that instant.

steps.description: Differentiate x with respect to t
steps.explanation: Differentiating the horizontal position gives the horizontal velocity $\dot{x}$. This becomes the denominator when we build the path's gradient.

steps.description: Differentiate y with respect to t
steps.explanation: Differentiating the vertical position gives the vertical velocity $\dot{y}$. This becomes the numerator of the gradient.

steps.description: Form the gradient of the path
steps.explanation: The chain rule links the two rates: dividing the vertical rate by the horizontal rate cancels the $dt$ and leaves the slope of the path in the $x$-$y$ plane.
The tangent touches the path at the drone's actual location, so we substitute $t = 2$ into the position equations to get the point $(3,\,2)$.
Putting $t = 2$ into the gradient formula gives the exact slope of the path at that moment, which is the slope of the tangent line.

steps.description: Write the tangent in point-slope form
steps.explanation: A straight line through $(3,\,2)$ with gradient $\dfrac{9}{4}$ is written most directly in point-slope form before tidying.
Multiplying both sides by $4$ removes the fraction, which makes it easy to collect everything on one side.
Moving all terms to one side gives the tidy implicit form $ax + by + c = 0$ that the question asks for.
This line passes through $(3,\,2)$ with gradient $\dfrac{9}{4}$, matching both conditions, so it is the tangent to the path at $t = 2$.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The tangent to the path at $t = 2$ is $9x - 4y - 19 = 0$ (equivalently $y = \dfrac{9}{4}x - \dfrac{19}{4}$).
